comparisons.visual: Moonlight spills into a rocky den in the Seeonee hills. FATHER WOLF, a lean gray wolf, wakes from his day's rest, scratches, yawns, and spreads his paws one aft…
Below in the valley that runs to a little river: the dry, angry, snarly, singsong whine of a tiger who has caught nothing.
SHERE KHAN, huge lame tiger, tumbles in the scrub, muttering, feet scorched. Tabaqui dances near him.
Moonlight at the mouth is blocked. Shere Khan's great square head and shoulders thrust into the entrance. Tabaqui squeaks behind him.
Mother Wolf throws herself down panting among the cubs.
No answer. Mother Wolf gathers herself for a last fight. BALOO, a sleepy brown bear, rises on his hindquarters and grunts.
Mowgli sighs, climbs a low branch, drops, runs a circle, swims a hand through a pool's edge as if rehearsing.
Mowgli answers with a long, perfect hiss. He kicks up his heels, claps, jumps sideways onto Bagheera's back, drums with his heels on the glossy hide, and makes …
Mowgli looks to Bagheera. The panther's eyes are hard as jade.
Two strong monkeys catch Mowgli under the arms and swing off through the treetops, twenty feet at a bound. The whole tribe sweeps along the tree-roads with Mowg…
Baloo and Bagheera crash through undergrowth, furious and spent. Rann stoops and cries the news.
Baloo and Bagheera burst from the jungle. Monkeys scream and fling themselves upon them. Bagheera fights toward the tank; Baloo stands like a rock under a rain …
The curve of the broken dome hangs above Mowgli. Cobras recoil in the dust.
He holds out his hands.
All move again. Mowgli lays his hands on Baloo and Bagheera. The two great beasts start as if waked from a dream.
Mowgli lays his head on Bagheera's back and sleeps as they move into the dark.
The valley opens into a great plain dotted with rocks and cut by ravines. At one end a little village; at the other the thick jungle stops as if cut with a hoe.…
Mowgli divides the herd-Akela taking the big bulls one way, Gray Brother the cows and calves the other-trapping Shere Khan, heavy with a village meal, in the na…
Messua flees back toward the houses.
Moonlight turns the plain milky white. Far behind, temple bells bang and conches blare. Messua's distant weeping carries on the wind.
From habit the Pack answers. Lame wolves, shot-wounded wolves, mangy wolves-many missing-drag themselves to the Rock. They stare at the striped hide, huge claws…
Miles of basalt rock and sand drowned in seals-hundreds of thousands. Bulls roar and fight in the breakers and on the nurseries. Surf dotted with heads hurrying…
Dark water. Kotick follows the Sea Cow through a long drowned tunnel beneath the cliffs, lungs burning, until-
The others laugh with him.
Kotick roars in answer. Sea Catch waddles in, mustache on end, blowing like a locomotive. Matkah and the seal that will marry Kotick cower down and admire their…
A week later. Kotick leads his army-nearly ten thousand holluschickie and old seals-away north toward the Sea Cow's tunnel. Seals that stay at Novastoshnah call…
Hot sun. A small draggled mongoose lies senseless on the middle of a garden path-fur like a little cat, head and habits like a weasel, eyes and restless nose pi…
They feed him raw meat. Rikki-tikki sleeps in Teddy's nursery that night on the boy's pillow.
Rikki-tikki hears a dry slither. Nag has entered through the sluice to wait for Teddy's Father in the morning. Rikki waits on the rim of the water jar. When Nag…
She snatches the egg and flies for the rat-hole by the melon bed. Rikki follows her down into the dark.
He settles again, proud but not too proud, eyes already half shut.
Through the trees moves a great elephant, nearly seventy years old, ten fair feet at the shoulders: KALA NAG-Black Snake-tusks cut short and bound with copper b…
In a lull he slides down, slips between the elephants, and throws up the loose end of a rope to a driver struggling with a kicking young calf. Kala Nag sees him…
Petersen Sahib turns.
Another roar of laughter-the old joke that means never. Kala Nag sets Little Toomai down. The boy bows to the earth again.
Little Toomai leads Petersen Sahib and Machua Appa to the beaten ground-the dance-floor stamped hard as a road. The men stare. Petersen Sahib looks long at the …
She draws the cloth closer about the child.
Through the murk: the AMIR OF AFGHANISTAN rides with a bodyguard of eight hundred savage men and horses who stare at locomotives and tent-streets as at miracles…
Roaring, grunting, bubbling. The tent-pole snaps. The whole tent caves and dances like a mad ghost as a big baggage camel blunders through it. NARRATOR, wet and…
Billy cocks one ear.
